notes: 14 clues covering Rules & Terminology, Baseball, Tennis & Golf, Board Games & Card Games, Sports Records & Awards. Weighted toward $800–$1000 (UNDER-WEIGHT tier). Two $1000 clues: Chicago White Sox origin and Garry Kasparov. Sources from Wikipedia via build-topic-docs.

questions.clue: This tabular game summary — common to baseball, basketball, and hockey — lists both individual player statistics and team achievements in a condensed column format.

citations.title: Box score
citations.snippet: A box score is a structured summary of the results from a sport competition. The box score lists the game score as well as individual and team achievements in the game.

questions.clue: This sporting term — applied across soccer, cricket, hockey, and other disciplines — designates the achievement of any generally positive feat accomplished three times within a single match.

citations.title: Hat-trick
citations.snippet: A hat-trick or hat trick is the achievement of a generally positive feat three times in a match, or another achievement based on the number three.

questions.clue: In baseball and ice hockey, this term names the melee in which most or all players abandon their dugouts or bullpens to converge on the playing surface, typically in response to an altercation already underway.

citations.title: Bench-clearing brawl
citations.snippet: A bench-clearing brawl is a form of fighting that occurs in sports, most notably baseball and ice hockey, where most or all players on both teams leave their dugouts, bullpens, or benches, and charge onto the playing area in order to fight

questions.clue: Played on an oval field between two 11-player teams, this bat-and-ball sport centers on a 22-yard pitch flanked by wickets — each consisting of three wooden stumps topped by two removable bails.

citations.title: Cricket
citations.snippet: Cricket is a bat-and-ball game that is played between two teams of eleven players on a field, at the centre of which is a 22-yard pitch with a wicket at each end, each comprising two bails balanced on three stumps. Two players from the batt

questions.clue: This tile-based game for three or four players is typically played with 144 tiles inscribed with Chinese characters and symbols; each participant occupies one of four 'wind' positions — East, South, West, or North — and draws a starting hand of thirteen tiles.

citations.title: Mahjong
citations.snippet: Mahjong is a tile-based game for three to four players. Though regional variations may exclude certain tiles or add unique ones, it is typically played with a set of 144 tiles based on Chinese characters and symbols. Players hold one of fou

questions.clue: Pioneered at the 1968 Mexico City Olympics by an American high jumper who sprinted diagonally toward the bar and cleared it back-first, this technique revolutionized the event and has seen near-universal adoption ever since.

citations.title: Dick Fosbury
citations.snippet: He won a gold medal at the 1968 Summer Olympics, revolutionizing the high jump event with a 'back-first' technique now known as the Fosbury flop. His method was to sprint diagonally towards the bar, then curve and leap backward over it... This approach has seen nearly universal adoption since Fosbury's performance in Mexico.

questions.clue: In its strictest tennis sense, this achievement means winning all four major championships in a single calendar year; claiming all four across a career rather than within one year is called the 'Career' version.

citations.title: Grand Slam (tennis)
citations.snippet: The Grand Slam in tennis is the achievement of winning all four major championships in one discipline in a calendar year. In doubles, a Grand Slam may be achieved as a team or as an individual with different partners. Winning all four major

questions.clue: Born with the surname Sinclair, this American boxer adopted his father's ring name, claimed a 1996 Olympic bronze medal in featherweight, and went on to win 15 world championship belts across five weight classes.

citations.title: Floyd Mayweather Jr.
citations.snippet: Floyd Joy Mayweather Jr. is an American professional boxer and boxing promoter. He is undefeated at 50–0. Mayweather won 15 major world championships spanning five weight classes from super featherweight to light middleweight. This includes

questions.clue: This Olympic combat sport — contested with three weapons (foil, épée, and sabre) — once included a fourth discipline called singlestick at the 1904 Games before settling into its current three-weapon form.

citations.title: Fencing
citations.snippet: Fencing is a combat sport that features sword fighting. It consists of three primary disciplines: foil, épée, and sabre, each with its own blade and set of rules. Most competitive fencers specialise in one of these disciplines. The modern s

questions.clue: Contested in an all-deep pool between two seven-player teams, this aquatic sport requires athletes to tread continuously using the 'eggbeater kick,' as they are not permitted to touch the pool's floor during play.

citations.title: Water polo
citations.snippet: Water polo is a competitive team sport played in water between two teams of seven players each. The game consists of four quarters in which the teams attempt to score goals by throwing the ball into the opposing team's goal. The team with m

questions.clue: A relief pitcher earns this statistical credit — abbreviated SV — by finishing a winning game with a lead of three runs or fewer, or by entering with the tying run at the plate, on deck, or on base.

citations.title: Save (baseball)
citations.snippet: In baseball, a save is credited to a pitcher who finishes a game for the winning team under certain circumstances. A save can be earned by entering a game in which his team is leading by three or fewer runs and finishing the game by pitchin

questions.clue: Used primarily in aquatic sports to tread in place, this leg technique allows athletes to remain stationary and upright without touching the pool floor, keeping both arms free for passing and shooting.

questions.clue: Born Garik Weinstein in Baku in 1963, this chess grandmaster adopted his mother's Armenian surname before defeating Anatoly Karpov in 1985 to become the youngest undisputed world champion in the game's history, at age 22.

citations.title: Garry Kasparov
citations.snippet: Garry Kimovich Kasparov is a Russian chess grandmaster, political activist and writer, who was the World Chess Champion from 1985 to 2000. His peak FIDE chess rating of 2851, achieved in 1999, was the highest recorded until being surpassed

questions.clue: This MLB franchise — known as the 'South Siders' — traces its origins to 1894 as the Sioux City Cornhuskers before relocating through Saint Paul, Minnesota, and settling under its current name when it arrived in Illinois in 1900.

citations.title: Chicago White Sox
citations.snippet: The Chicago White Sox are an American professional baseball team based in Chicago. The White Sox compete in Major League Baseball (MLB) as a member club of the American League (AL) Central Division. The club plays its home games at Rate Fie
